phases.name: request-analysis
entryCriteria: A user request or product requirement exists.
qualityGates: spec has goal, scope, constraints, acceptance criteria, and open questions
tasks have owners, dependencies, and verification method
phases.rollback: Clarify the requirement with the requester and update spec.md.

phases.name: requirement-review
qualityGates: review result is APPROVED or REVISION REQUIRED
MUST FIX findings are resolved before implementation
phases.rollback: Return to phase 1 when the review result is REVISION REQUIRED.

phases.name: implementation
entryCriteria: Requirement review is APPROVED and user confirms the plan.
qualityGates: implementation follows layer boundaries
changed behavior maps to an accepted task
phases.rollback: Return to phase 1 for scope mismatch or phase 3 for compile errors.

phases.name: code-review
entryCriteria: Implementation notes and code changes are available.
qualityGates: review contains verdict, findings, and evidence
MUST FIX findings are resolved before tests are accepted
phases.rollback: Return to phase 3 when code review requires changes.

phases.name: unit-test-writing
entryCriteria: Code review is APPROVED or all MUST FIX findings are resolved.
qualityGates: tests are change-driven
critical branches and error paths are covered
phases.rollback: Return to phase 3 if code is not testable without unsafe refactoring.

phases.name: unit-test-review
entryCriteria: Test plan and test changes exist.
qualityGates: tests assert behavior instead of implementation details
tests fail for the targeted defect or missing behavior
phases.rollback: Return to phase 5 when test review requires changes.

phases.name: code-push-readiness
qualityGates: local validation command is documented
no unresolved MUST FIX finding remains
phases.rollback: Return to the phase that owns the unresolved finding.

phases.name: ci-verification
qualityGates: status == SUCCESS
totalTests > 0
passedTests == totalTests
phases.rollback: Return to phase 5 when totalTests is 0, phase 3 for compile errors, or the owning phase for failed assertions.

phases.name: deployment-verification
entryCriteria: CI verification passed and deployment parameters are confirmed.
qualityGates: environment is explicit
smoke checks pass
rollback plan is documented
phases.rollback: Return to phase 3 for functional failures or halt for environment incidents.

phases.name: user-confirmation
entryCriteria: Deployment verification passed or deployment is not required.
qualityGates: user-visible outcome is summarized
verification evidence is linked
follow-up harness improvements are captured
phases.rollback: Return to phase 1 for requirement mismatch or create a new change for follow-up work.

extensionStages.name: Government/Enterprise Prototype Confirmation
extensionStages.trigger: Use when stakeholders require high-fidelity prototype confirmation before business coding.
qualityGates: prototype covers MVP pages, flows, states, and acceptance criteria
prototype review confirms no scope creep before coding

extensionStages.name: Implementation Readiness Skeleton
extensionStages.trigger: Use before full business coding when contract, schema, scaffold, and runtime readiness need validation.
qualityGates: OpenAPI contract and database migration are stable enough for parallel work
backend, frontend, and dependency smoke checks are executable

extensionStages.name: Frontend Code/Test Review
extensionStages.trigger: Use when frontend pages, API clients, or high-fidelity prototype implementation changes.
qualityGates: frontend matches prototype, API contract, UX state requirements, and scope guardrails
frontend verification gaps are explicit before integration or acceptance

extensionStages.name: Front-Back E2E Integration Gate
extensionStages.trigger: Use when backend, frontend, database, and proxy/runtime integration must be proven together.
qualityGates: runtime dependencies, backend, frontend, and proxy are reachable
browser E2E covers critical happy path and at least one critical failure path

extensionStages.name: Acceptance Preparation and Final Delivery Audit
extensionStages.trigger: Use before formal delivery audit, government/enterprise UAT, or workflow validation sign-off.
qualityGates: MUST FIX, SHOULD FIX, and NICE TO HAVE findings are separated
final audit links requirements, design, code, tests, E2E, and executable validation evidence

extensionStages.name: SDD/OpenSpec Change Governance
extensionStages.trigger: Use when a change declares the SDD/OpenSpec/Superpowers governance version or needs auditable requirement, decision, context, and archive traceability.
qualityGates: loaded context, rules, code, documents, and decisions are recorded before design or implementation
proposal and plan separate goals, non-goals, risks, alternatives, validation, and rollback evidence
archive captures final decision, residual work, reuse lessons, and SHOULD FIX / NICE TO HAVE follow-up

extensionStages.name: TDD Execution Quality
extensionStages.trigger: Use for high-risk implementation changes involving money, state machines, security, permissions, databases, public APIs, deployment, or equivalent risk evidence.
qualityGates: Red-Green-Refactor evidence is retained or an equivalent executable testing rationale is documented
task execution order, ownership boundaries, and validation method are explicit
spec compliance review and code quality review are both retained and cannot replace base requirement, code, test, CI, E2E, or final audit gates

extensionStages.name: Lightweight Entry Governance
extensionStages.trigger: Use at change intake when the team needs a simple entry decision, configurable gate profile, or auditable process trimming evidence.
qualityGates: entry decision uses no more than five intake questions before selecting a profile
process trimming can combine evidence but cannot delete audit responsibility
low-risk changes keep Harness validation while high-risk changes inherit stricter riskRules

gateProfiles.name: Full Audit
qualityGates: all reviews are explicit
backend, frontend, E2E, CI, deploy, and acceptance evidence are retained
notUseCases: documentation-only or template-only updates without implementation risk
early exploration where formal acceptance is not required

gateProfiles.name: Balanced Enterprise
qualityGates: combine low-risk reviews without deleting reviewer evidence
keep executable backend/frontend/E2E gates for user-visible behavior
notUseCases: regulated or external acceptance that requires full traceability
money, security, database, public API, or deployment changes without risk-adaptive controls

gateProfiles.name: Fast Iteration
qualityGates: scope guard and basic smoke/build checks remain executable
deferred reviews and tests are tracked explicitly
notUseCases: money, state-machine, security, permission, database, public API, deployment, or external acceptance changes
changes that need formal UAT, audit, or production rollback evidence
requiredEvidence: verification/ci-result.json or equivalent Harness validate evidence

gateProfiles.name: Government/Enterprise Prototype First
qualityGates: prototype artifacts and prototype review are complete before business coding
frontend review checks prototype consistency before acceptance
notUseCases: internal-only changes with no prototype sign-off need

gateProfiles.name: Risk Adaptive
qualityGates: risk tags decide review depth and verification command set
money, state transition, security, API, database, and deployment changes cannot skip review
notUseCases: teams without agreed risk tags or risk-rule ownership
single-path regulated delivery where full-audit is simpler

gateProfiles.name: TDD Execution
qualityGates: TDD cycle log, task execution plan, spec compliance review, and code quality review are retained
TDD execution adds evidence and never replaces requirement review, code review, test review, CI, E2E, or final audit
notUseCases: documentation, template-only, process-copy, or dry-run changes
low-risk UI copy or non-behavioral changes with no implementation risk

qualityGates: money and financial calculations cannot use validate-only gates
financial behavior must keep executable tests, TDD evidence, and acceptance evidence
state transition rules must be reviewed independently
critical transition paths must have executable regression tests and TDD cycle evidence
security and permission changes cannot skip review evidence
authorization behavior must be covered by executable validation and TDD evidence
database changes require migration readiness evidence
data migration risk must include rollback or recovery guidance and executable test evidence
API contract changes require contract readiness evidence
frontend/backend compatibility must be executable or explicitly stubbed and covered by execution evidence
runtime changes require environment readiness or deploy verification evidence
rollback plan must be documented before acceptance and high-risk execution evidence must be retained
prototype or acceptance evidence must be retained before formal UAT
MUST FIX, SHOULD FIX, and NICE TO HAVE findings must be separated
documentation-only changes still require Harness validation
documentation, template-only, process-copy, and dry-run changes are not forced through TDD execution quality gates
deferred review or verification work must be tracked explicitly
